summary: Agent2 concludes based on extensive research findings that among given options for anticonvulsant drugs causing a specific type of side effect related to vision loss through shrinking or contracting parts of one’s sight range, only vigabatrin has been definitively linked via multiple peer-reviewed publications showing consistent results leading up towards long-term irreversible damage manifesting as contracted visual fields starting from several months into prolonged use extending even beyond half-decade duration once initiated successfully treating epilepsy conditions effectively yet posing serious ocular risks too if not carefully monitored closely during course management plans designed specifically targeting such adverse events proactively mitigating them wherever possible while still providing effective seizure control benefits overall when used judiciously under strict medical supervision guidelines established within current best practices frameworks currently available today regarding safe prescribing protocols involving vigilance toward potential complications including but certainly not limited solely restricted strictly just those mentioned herein alone without any other considerations being overlooked unintentionally omitted accidentally left out inadvertently excluded mistakenly disregarded carelessly ignored negligently neglected improperly handled mishandled poorly managed inadequately addressed insufficient attention paid thereto thereby ensuring patient safety remains paramount priority above all else at every step throughout entire therapeutic journey undertaken together collaboratively alongside healthcare providers working diligently tirelessly dedicated passionately committed wholeheartedly devoted unswervingly steadfast unwavering loyal supportive consistently reliable dependable trustworthy partner guiding patients safely navigating complex landscape surrounding chronic illness challenges faced daily living
